description_long: This object is a handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie. It features a long, slender antenna extending from the top, which is essential for signal transmission and reception. The front face displays a numeric keypad, several physical buttons, and a small speaker grille, likely for audio output. The upper section includes rotary knobs for power and volume control, as well as a display screen for channel or status information. The body is constructed from a dark, likely ruggedized plastic, designed to withstand outdoor or urban use. The compact, rectangular form and ergonomic design make it suitable for portable communication in various urban environments.
description: A rugged, handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, numeric keypad, and physical control knobs, designed for portable communication.
description_view_0: Front view showing the numeric keypad, speaker grille, display screen, and control knobs, with the antenna extending upward.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile and the protruding antenna, with minimal visible controls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid back panel with the antenna and top controls partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a similarly slim profile, with a possible side button or port visible.
